enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a video request from Nathan Washington about what travel and tourism will look like in the 2060s. He envisions a world where the planet becomes a 'giant playground' with richly planned historical and adventure tourism — recreating ancient trade routes, Lewis and Clark expeditions, Apache raids, and battle reenactments. He also shares his current project of building KMZ files for Google Earth to map Boulder's trails, historical villages, and ancient routes, and speculates on improved weather forecasting, better oxygen tech for high-altitude climbs, and eventually moon and Mars tourism.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Travel will become highly purposeful and experiential, recreating historical routes and events with period-accurate methods
America is one of the best places in the world for river cruising but people don't realize it yet
Combining protective knowledge and nature together is a beautiful life
KMZ is the only way to share geographic routes because it lets people actually explore in Google Earth rather than just see a static image
Boulder has an incredible and underappreciated history and tourism potential
Anti-gravity ships, moon trips, and Mars sand dune races are plausible by then

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: This is my first video request since I started doing video requests again from a dude named Nathan Washington Perm up from up in Seattle, Washington.

claims.claim: I think we'll pretty much have turned the planet into a big old giant fucking playground.

claims.claim: I'm working on a website that's pretty much kind of like catering to that kind of planning on that.

claims.claim: I'm building on collecting KML files and Google Earth KML files so that you can see all the traces and everything on Google Earth.

claims.subject: Boulder
claims.claim: Boulder's a good place to practice with that because to start it out kind of turn Boulder into its tourist destination because there's just so much around here to look at and to do.

claims.subject: future activities
claims.claim: People will go somewhere to do stuff like skiing all the glaciers, runs across all the sand dunes, kayak all the rivers, hike up all the little roots and the flattards.

claims.subject: ancient tours
claims.claim: There will be tours of ancient mining in Boulder county, tours of the villages that existed in 1875, tours of the villages that existed in 1950 kind of stuff.

claims.subject: ancient trade routes
claims.claim: You could recreate all of the tours that anybody ever did, like the ancient trade route from down the Tigris and the Euphrates all the way from Greece to Babylon.

claims.subject: Pheidippides
claims.claim: Pheidippides ran from Athens to Sparta in a day and a half, 150 miles, and then he died the next day.

claims.subject: Apache
claims.claim: There's lots of cool stuff draw rates that the ancient Apaches did like Geronimo rates like when they go and capture ladies on the wagon train and then take them back to their chief like 300 miles away.

claims.subject: buffalo roots
claims.claim: You can make buffalo root by path facing the buffalo root all right up bird migration by path for all the different birds.

claims.subject: bike path
claims.claim: I want to have like a road for bicycle racers to ride from Boulder all the way to the Atlantic Ocean like without having to stop just on one bike path.

claims.subject: Antarctica
claims.claim: People will be going across Antarctica skiing across Antarctica and the Arctic.

claims.subject: kayaking
claims.claim: I want to do a kayaking trip from my house down the river all the way down to the Pacific or the Gulf of Mexico.

claims.subject: Oregon trail
claims.claim: When the wagon train would go across on the Oregon trail a lot of the wagoners tried to put their wagons on boats and try to take their boats down the river because it was easier.

claims.subject: Lewis and Clark
claims.claim: Lewis and Clark a lot of their trip when they went from America to the Coast of Pacific and back in 1805 as part of Jefferson's ordered it because we bought America and the Louisiana purchase from Napoleon for like $3 million.

claims.subject: America
claims.claim: America is a great river place. I don't think people realized it yet, but if you're going to compare America to the rest of the world, America would be one of the best places to do river cruises.

claims.subject: future tourism
claims.claim: With YouTube and Google Earth, people can enjoy that stuff vicariously, you know, like people can be really into it without actually doing it.

claims.subject: mountain climbing
claims.claim: In the future, we'll have the science of the weather science figured out so much that you'll know when to go up when there won't be a storm, you won't get blindsided by any storms.

claims.subject: oxygen tanks
claims.claim: People will just have better oxygen tanks so you can just put on you don't have to worry about dying.

claims.subject: surfing
claims.claim: You just put up with a little with a little hot oxygen thing on your mask so you can get from the water forever but you can keep it.

claims.subject: future trips
claims.claim: You can start all that stuff right that cool designing trips and stuff and sharing it with on your computer and your GoPro and your energy you need to without even having to go anywhere.

claims.subject: future lifestyle
claims.claim: What I want to do is like every day going like a bike ride or a new bike ride a really big bike ride as soon as I get caught up on my website.

claims.subject: ancient Rome
claims.claim: With my sanctimonian thing like may oh we're at ancient Rome like what time would you be an ancient in ancient Rome?

claims.claim: People will even be touching relics will organize your Alex name in the future private owners will sell that relics back to the cup companies you know countries like maybe countries will go out the program.

claims.subject: future jobs
claims.claim: That provides jobs because people people can guide people people love having guides you want to have someone go show you the way and the support liquid and also teachers.

claims.claim: By then we'll have it all figured out you know, I think we'll have it figured out really pretty soon.

claims.subject: aliens

claims.claim: I imagine how big the water slides will be oh my god.
